d615faf4-28ea-43a5-91d2-1e93a7667010.title: Create unified test artifacts folder and document target HDF5 schema
d615faf4-28ea-43a5-91d2-1e93a7667010.description: Create con vert/test_artifacts/ folder. Document the 16 streams (4 frame rates: 60/50/20/10 Hz), 10 vector + 3 image + 3 gripper groups, per-stream timestamps format, and verify all 179 JPEGs have valid SOI/EOI markers. Write schema.json capturing all metadata needed by downstream converters.

success_criteria.text: Folder convert/test_artifacts/ exists with schema.json documenting all 16 streams
schema.json includes: per-stream name/type/dims/dtype/columns/encoding/frame_count
Target HDF5 at known relative path from project root

deliverables.text: convert/test_artifacts/README.md — describes the 4-rate async stream challenge

d615faf4-28ea-43a5-91d2-1e93a7667010.notes:
The target HDF5 has ASYNCHRONOUS streams: VR@60Hz(1119), IK@50Hz(820), arm@20Hz(358), camera@10Hz(179). Timestamps are uint64 nanosecond epoch, NOT frame-aligned. Each stream has independent timestamps. This is critical for converter design.

Generated schema.json from live HDF5 inspection: 16 streams, 4 frame rates, all JPEGs valid, all timestamps monotonic. Source path is relative from project root.

eb593dcb-f20c-4a59-b0d1-2468544478d7.title: Implement HDF5 to JSON Lines converter with output validation
eb593dcb-f20c-4a59-b0d1-2468544478d7.description: Build convert_h5_to_jsonl.py that reads the AIRS HDF5 file and writes one JSON line per frame. Must handle async streams: each line is a frame from the fastest stream (VR@1119), other streams include nearest-timestamp values. Include --streams filter to convert individual streams. Validate output: check line count matches frame count, verify JPEG base64 roundtrip, check timestamps monotonic.

success_criteria.text: convert_h5_to_jsonl.py converts target HDF5 to .jsonl with 1119 lines
Output .jsonl can be re-read with json.loads() on every line
--streams vr_head_pose converts only that single stream
JPEG base64 roundtrip produces identical bytes to source HDF5
Timestamps in output are strictly monotonic

eb593dcb-f20c-4a59-b0d1-2468544478d7.dependencies: Needs schema.json from d615faf4 for stream names, frame counts, and dimension metadata used during JSONL conversion.
eb593dcb-f20c-4a59-b0d1-2468544478d7.notes:
JSON Lines is the simplest target format — one JSON object per line. It validates the read path works correctly before tackling binary formats. Serves as the canary for all other converters.

JSONL converter passes all 5 criteria: 1119 lines, all parseable, single-stream filter works, JPEG roundtrip verified (22057B match), timestamps monotonic.

0e4d9258-fc7d-4e63-8954-f112acd34df2.title: Implement HDF5 to Parquet converter with per-stream files
0e4d9258-fc7d-4e63-8954-f112acd34df2.description: Build convert_h5_to_parquet.py that writes each AIRS stream to a separate .parquet file. Vector streams: schema (timestamp_ns: uint64, dim_0..dim_N: float32) with Zstd compression level 3. Image streams: schema (timestamp_ns: uint64, jpeg_bytes: binary). Include --consolidate flag to merge all streams into one Parquet file with a stream_name column. Validate: row count matches HDF5, column count matches dims, JPEG roundtrip identical, no NaN/Inf leakage.

success_criteria.text: convert_h5_to_parquet.py produces 16 .parquet files from target HDF5
Each vector .parquet has correct row count matching HDF5 frame count
parquet file row_counts: vr_*=1119, ik_*=820, arm_*=358, camera_*=179
pyarrow.parquet.read_table() roundtrips all float32 values to 1e-6 tolerance
JPEG bytes in Parquet binary column identical to HDF5 source

0e4d9258-fc7d-4e63-8954-f112acd34df2.dependencies: Needs schema.json from task d615faf4 for stream metadata (names, dims, types)
0e4d9258-fc7d-4e63-8954-f112acd34df2.notes: 16 Parquet files generated, all row counts verified (vr_*=1119, ik_*=820, arm_*=358, camera_*=179). Float32 roundtrip within 1e-6. JPEG bytes identical. Zstd level 3 compression.

08be2dc0-d19a-4ea9-9bec-d7eb4e27bb97.title: Implement HDF5 to Zarr converter preserving group hierarchy
08be2dc0-d19a-4ea9-9bec-d7eb4e27bb97.description: Build convert_h5_to_zarr.py that creates a Zarr store mirroring the AIRS HDF5 group hierarchy. Vector streams stored as (N, dims) float32 arrays chunked (1000, dims) with Blosc Zstd level-3 compression. Image streams stored as (N,) object arrays with VLenUTF8 codec for JPEG bytes. Copy all group attrs including type, columns, and encoding from HDF5 to Zarr.

success_criteria.text: Output Zarr store has 16 groups matching source HDF5 exactly
zarr.open() returns arrays with shape and dtype identical to HDF5 source
All group-level attrs (type, columns, encoding) roundtrip from HDF5 to Zarr
Reading data[100:200] slice from Zarr equals HDF5 data[100:200] to 1e-6 tolerance

08be2dc0-d19a-4ea9-9bec-d7eb4e27bb97.dependencies: Needs schema.json from task d615faf4 for stream group hierarchy and dims

c2d12fe2-d1e9-4a42-addf-f1e9622fa495.title: Implement HDF5 to LeRobot v3 converter
c2d12fe2-d1e9-4a42-addf-f1e9622fa495.description: Build convert_h5_to_lerobot.py that converts the AIRS HDF5 to LeRobot v3 format. Map AIRS vector streams to observation.state.* and action.* with feature namespaces. Encode JPEG streams to MP4 video per camera using AV1 codec. Write meta/info.json, meta/stats.json, meta/episodes/ Parquet files. Handle multi-rate async streams by selecting fastest VR stream (1119 frames) as canonical timeline, other streams interpolated or nearest-neighbor matched.

success_criteria.text: Output passes lerobot dataset validation with 0 errors
dataset[0] returns frame dict with observation.state and observation.images keys
MP4 video files for 3 cameras each have exactly 179 frames
stats.json contains mean/std/min/max for all vector features
episodes metadata Parquet records exactly 1 episode with 1119 frames

c2d12fe2-d1e9-4a42-addf-f1e9622fa495.dependencies: Needs schema.json (d615faf4) for stream metadata. Needs JSONL converter (eb593dcb) to validate read path correctness before binary format work.

fc1f23ec-5bcd-4bc6-a5b9-715a4f5dce53.title: Build output validation tool for cross-format content integrity
fc1f23ec-5bcd-4bc6-a5b9-715a4f5dce53.description: Build validate_conversion.py that compares converted output against source HDF5. For each target format: check frame counts match, verify float32 values within 1e-6 tolerance, verify JPEG bytes roundtrip losslessly, verify timestamps are monotonic and within original range, check no NaN/Inf introduced. Produce a structured ValidationReport JSON. Run on all test_artifacts/ outputs.

success_criteria.text: Validation tool produces a JSON report with per-format pass/fail status
Validates Parquet output: 16 files, correct row counts per stream
Validates Zarr output: 16 groups, matching shapes and dtypes
Validates JSONL output: 1119 lines, all parseable, JPEG base64 decodes correctly
Validates LeRobot output: video frames have correct count, feature schema matches

fc1f23ec-5bcd-4bc6-a5b9-715a4f5dce53.dependencies: Can only validate outputs after all converters (Parquet 0e4d9258, Zarr 08be2dc0, JSONL eb593dcb, LeRobot c2d12fe2) have produced their test artifacts.

209be930-37cd-4669-b0fd-054c64888a6b.title: Implement folder-to-consolidated converter for batch HDF5 episodes
209be930-37cd-4669-b0fd-054c64888a6b.description: Build convert_folder.py that takes a directory of AIRS .h5 episode files and consolidates them into a single target format (Parquet or Zarr). Adds episode_id column to distinguish source episodes. Handles variable-length streams across episodes (different recording durations). Produces one output per stream type with concatenated frames from all episodes.

success_criteria.text: convert_folder.py --format parquet processes multiple .h5 files into single .parquet per stream
Output Parquet files have episode_id column with correct per-episode values
convert_folder.py --format zarr produces consolidated Zarr store with episode_index array
Tool reports per-episode frame counts and total consolidated frames in stdout

209be930-37cd-4669-b0fd-054c64888a6b.dependencies: Needs single-file Parquet (0e4d9258) and Zarr (08be2dc0) converters working first to reuse their per-stream write logic.

c62ce026-deab-472f-9a32-1b43dca85219.title: Write convert/README.md documenting the full conversion pipeline
c62ce026-deab-472f-9a32-1b43dca85219.description: Write convert/README.md explaining: (1) the AIRS HDF5 multi-rate async stream structure, (2) each target format and when to use it, (3) CLI usage for each converter tool, (4) the validation tool, (5) expected output directory layout. Include example commands for each converter using the bundled test artifact.

success_criteria.text: README.md documents all 5 converter tools with CLI examples
README.md explains the 4-rate async stream challenge and how each format handles it
Format comparison table included with Parquet/Zarr/JSONL/LeRobot columns

c62ce026-deab-472f-9a32-1b43dca85219.dependencies: Write README after validation tool (fc1f23ec) confirms all converters produce correct output, so the README can include verified example outputs.
